fr.name: Un modèle consacré aux destinations résilientes pour la reprise du tourisme: moins c'est plus
fr.description: La pandémie a représenté un choc pour le tourisme:le secteur est affecté par une crise qui fait basculer les domaines socio-financiers. L'impact se fait sentir dans l'ensemble du système touristique, ainsi seule une approche conjointe transnationale peut poursuivre la réouverture des destinations tout en consolidant la coopération publique et privée. Les territoires saisissent les opportunités pour faire face à la crise en favorisant une économie du tourisme résiliente et alternative dans le respect des écosystèmes et du patrimoine naturel. ConsumeLess Plus renforce le tourisme à l’égard du Green Deal, en réduisant au maximum la production de déchets et la consommation d'eau et d'énergie dans l’effort conjoint d’un tourisme plus compétitif, innovant et inclusif. ConsumeLess Plus va transférer à Chypre, en Croatie, en France et au Monténégro un modèle prêt à l'emploi et une étiquette souple, incluant une campagne de communication et de marché du territoire, ainsi qu'un réseau méditerranéen de destinations ConsumelessMed. Le projet soutient 4 nouvelles régions à devenir des destinations ConsumelessMed en mettant en place le conseil local et en impliquant des PME pour autoévaluer leur propre chemin vers un développement commercial plus innovant et durable.La leçon apprise avec le modèle testé auparavant dans 5 régions Med est capitalisée pour avancer en tant que réseau et communauté qui prend soin de l'économie locale, des écosystèmes, du bien-être des citoyens et des voyageurs.
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d'instruments disponibles pour améliorer le développement de tourisme durable et responsable, Nombre de destinations touristiques couvertes par un outil d'évaluation de tourisme durable, Nombre de stratégies appliquant des critères de gestion de tourisme durables, Nombre de régions et des sous-régions engagées (par des chartes, des protocoles, le MoU) dans la mise en œuvre de plans de tourisme durables, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 3,

en.name: A model 4 resilient destinations toward tourism recovery: Less is more
en.description: The Covid-19 pandemic has represented a critical blow for the tourism: the sector is severely affected by an unprecedented crisis pushing over social and financial structures. The impact is being felt within the entire tourism system, then solely a joined-up approach at transnational level is able to go ahead reopening and rebuilding destinations while enhancing public and private cooperation. Territories seize the opportunities to face the crisis fostering a more resilient and alternative tourism’s economy, meanwhile respecting ecosystems and valorising natural heritage.ConsumeLess Plus strengthens the tourism sector contributing to the Green Deal, minimizing waste production and water and energy use, while boosting joint effort toward a more competitive, innovative and inclusive tourism. ConsumeLess Plus is going to transfer in Cyprus, Croatia, France and Montenegro a ready-to-use model and “light” label, including an innovative communication and territory-based marketing campaign, together with a Mediterranean network of ConsumelessMed destinations. The project supports 4 new regions to become ConsumelessMed destinations setting up the local board, involving SMEs for self-assessing their own pathway toward a more sustainable and innovative business development. The lesson learnt, testing the model already in 5 Med regions, is capitalised to act as a network and think as a community which takes care of local economy, ecosystems, wellbeing of citizens and travellers.
en.expected_results: 1 Toward the acknowledge of all Mediterranean coastal regions for the sustainability of their destinations : ConsumeLess model contributes to the promotion of smart and sustainable growth in Mediterranean coastal regions through public and private collaboration, to jointly promote the territories at international level. The ConsumeLess destinations enhance their attractiveness and competitiveness, while preserving ecosystem services, minimising resource use (e.g.: energy and water), reducing the amount of wastes generated, minimising emissions and pollution, ensuring well-being to the citizens and visitors. Furthermore, the ConsumeLess model has been created, tested and assessed to be accessible and easy transferable to all actors, public or private, small or medium-large, competent on environmental related issues or not. Public actors and the local business community primarily agree on a common will: be a sustainable destination that is able to valorise contemporary local culture and economy, enhancing local manufacturing and contributing to growth! They work together as a network, sharing the easy model’s commitments according to their role and capacity. Improving the level of sustainability of the 4 new Med regions from Croatia, Montenegro, France and Cyprus, in one year, is possible thanks to the ready-to-use ConsumeLess model (including manual, guideline, label, protocols, tutorials, handbook, promotional products, marketing strategy, platform for promotion… ), and taking into account, the will of these regions to join this Mediterranean community to growth together.1 Toward the acknowledge of all Mediterranean coastal regions for the sustainability of their destinations : ConsumeLess model contributes to the promotion of smart and sustainable growth in Mediterranean coastal regions through public and private collaboration, to jointly promote the territories at international level. The ConsumeLess destinations enhance their attractiveness and competitiveness, while preserving ecosystem services, minimising resource use (e.g.: energy and water), reducing the amount of wastes generated, minimising emissions and pollution, ensuring well-being to the citizens and visitors. Furthermore, the ConsumeLess model has been created, tested and assessed to be accessible and easy transferable to all actors, public or private, small or medium-large, competent on environmental related issues or not. Public actors and the local business community primarily agree on a common will: be a sustainable destination that is able to valorise contemporary local culture and economy, enhancing local manufacturing and contributing to growth! They work together as a network, sharing the easy model’s commitments according to their role and capacity. Improving the level of sustainability of the 4 new Med regions from Croatia, Montenegro, France and Cyprus, in one year, is possible thanks to the ready-to-use ConsumeLess model (including manual, guideline, label, protocols, tutorials, handbook, promotional products, marketing strategy, platform for promotion… ), and taking into account, the will of these regions to join this Mediterranean community to growth together.
en.achievements: The givers, EWA & AI, from the start have supported Receivers to understand the model & activities to be developed, & transfer the good practices in their own territories. In particular during the first 6 months, they organised 2 webinars (28/07/2021, 20/09/2021) involving partners, associated partners and possible members of the Local Committee (LC) invited by receiver partners. The webinars were necessary to make receivers aware about activities relating to the customization of the ConsumeLess tools & products, assessment of action plans, launch of the model at local level, involvement of facilities, communication, marketing strategy & organization of the Atypical communication event, linking sustainability with tourism recovery. EWA & AI supported through periodic online meetings to clarify doubts & identify the best strategy to be put in place for the action plan, and the actions to be implemented for having a larger outreach & thus higher impact (ex: communication campaign & demonstrative measures). Givers reviewed each document, assessed & customised by receivers, including the MoU signed by local members, the Action plans customised by receivers, their members and the translated documents to be published on the website & platform. Receivers worked constantly to identify & potentially involve members of the LC, by meeting & involving them during the local webinars to explain the model & actions to be developed, along with the commitments required from the members. Finally, the members customised & signed the MoU amounting to a total of 31 members, within 4 new territories, a very good result. Receivers customised the model & related documents, to be shared & discussed with the LCs' members, thus assessing the action plans. The 2 related outputs can be considered closed, producing: Output 3.1: Local Committee: closed with 31 entities involved as members of the 4 LCs & Output 3.2: Action plan & annex: closed with 4 assessed Action Plans, model&guideline translated & shared with members.All partners participated in relevant external events to promote Consumeless+ (ex: CCINice during the Food Hôtel Tech), opening a tailored webpage on their institutional channel and started promoting the project by social channels (locally and at project level). Communication products related to the project & the model, were customised with new project logo, new partner logos, & were shared with partners. The Roll-Up & poster were also edited and produced.
en.expected_outputs: ConsumeLess Local Committee - LC :4,Tailored ConsumeLess model and action plan :4,ConsumeLess Med voluntary agreements with facilities :4
en.delivered_outputs: Number of instruments available to enhance the development of sustainable and responsible tourism, Number of tourist destinations covered by a sustainable tourism evaluation tool, Number of strategies applying sustainable tourism management criteria, Number of regions and sub-regions engaged (through charters, protocols, MoU) in implementing sustainable tourism plans, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 3,

fr.name: Atteindre de nouveaux territoires grâce à l'utilisation d'outils d'aide à la gestion intégrée pour l'efficacité énergétique dans les bâtiments publics méditerranéens
fr.description: reIMPULSE transférera à de nouvelles régions et villes, les produits développés lors du précédent projet MED IMPULSE. Cela comprend la fourniture d'outils de soutien pour l'élaboration de plans de rénovation progressive et la planification financière pour des solutions optimales en coûts pour le parc immobilier public. L'outil d'évaluation du schéma financier, y compris l'outil d'aide à la décision PLUG-IN KPIs-processor pour la hiérarchie automatisée des bâtiments publics, seront révisés/adaptés de manière transnationaux pour aider les territoires à atteindre les nouveaux objectifs fixés par l'UE en la stratégie Green Deal et Renovation Wave. Ces derniers visent à doubler les taux annuels de rénovation énergétique et à rendre compte de la nécessité de rénover en profondeur les bâtiments à grande échelle. Le processus permettra, d'étendre le champ d'application dans certains des pays précédemment impliqués (Espagne, Grèce et Italie) des petites / moyennes municipalités aux grandes villes (comme Valence en Espagne), mais aussi, de l'échelle locale à l'échelle régionale (Région Émilie-Romagne en Italie et Grèce occidentale). En revanche, les résultats seront transférés vers un pays qui n'était pas impliqué auparavant dans IMPULSE (Slovénie). Il permettra à d'autres municipalités/régions de Slovénie d'utiliser les outils. Enfin, reIMPULSE permettra d'améliorer et d'adapter les outils aux exigences établies dans la nouvelle période de programmation de l'UE.
fr.expected_outputs: Protocole d’accord sur l'adoption du protocole pour la planification de la rénovation énergétique des bâtiments publics et l'évaluation du financement :4
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d'outils de planification disponibles pour le suivi et la gestion de la consommation énergétique dans les bâtiments publics, Nombre de stratégies en faveur des plans de gestion de la consommation énergétique dans les bâtiments publics, Nombre de groupes cibles participant aux activités de sensibilisation sur l'efficacité énergétique pour les bâtiments publics, Nombre de régions et sous-régions engagées (par le biais de chartes, protocoles, MoU) à développer des plans/stratégies d'efficacité énergétique, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 2,

en.name: Reaching new territories in the use of Integrated Management Support tools for Energy efficiency in Mediterranean PUblic buiLdings
en.description: reIMPULSE will effectively transfer to new regions and cities the main outputs developed during the previous MED project IMPULSE. This includes the provision of support tools for the development of gradual renovation plans and financial planning for cost-optimal solutions for public building stocks. The Financial Scheme Evaluation tool, including the decision-making support tool PLUG-IN KPIs-processor for automated hierarchy of public buildings, will be revised and adapted under a transnational and cooperative way to help territories to meet the new targets set by the EU in the Green Deal and Renovation Wave Strategy, which aim to double annual energy renovation rates in the next ten years and report on the need to renovate buildings deeply on a massive scale.The transferring process will allow, on the one hand, to scale the scope of application in some of the countries previously involved in IMPULSE project (Spain, Greece and Italy) from small/medium municipalities to bigger cities (such as Valencia in Spain), but also, from the local to the regional scale (Emilia Romagna Region in Italy and Western Greece). On the other hand, results will be transferred to a country that was not involved previously in IMPULSE (Slovenia). It will enable other municipalities/regions in Slovenia to make use of the tools taking the Municipality of Koper as a reference. Finally, reIMPULSE will permit to improve and adapt the tools to the requirements established in the new EU program period.
en.expected_results: 1 MoU by receivers’ territories on the adoption of the protocol followed for public buildings’ energy renovation planning and financing asse : Four MoUs among the project and the public entities responsible for the energy management and planning of public buildings of the targeted regions/municipalities will be signed. The MoUs will include the energy renovation plan for the stock of public buildings obtained automatically through the planning tool (PLUG-IN KPIs processor tool) adapted to each of the receivers’ territories, as well as the most appropriate financing scheme for that specific plan among the various solutions evaluated with the Financial schemes tool. In addition, a detailed protocol to follow will also be included, based on the lessons learned during the process of improvement, implementation and use of the tools, which will ensure effective take-up by the new territories.1 MoU by receivers’ territories on the adoption of the protocol followed for public buildings’ energy renovation planning and financing asse : Four MoUs among the project and the public entities responsible for the energy management and planning of public buildings of the targeted regions/municipalities will be signed. The MoUs will include the energy renovation plan for the stock of public buildings obtained automatically through the planning tool (PLUG-IN KPIs processor tool) adapted to each of the receivers’ territories, as well as the most appropriate financing scheme for that specific plan among the various solutions evaluated with the Financial schemes tool. In addition, a detailed protocol to follow will also be included, based on the lessons learned during the process of improvement, implementation and use of the tools, which will ensure effective take-up by the new territories.
en.achievements: IMPULSE PLUS project activities include those linked to management, communication issues, and the activities related to the transferring of the IMPULSE tools and methodology.The main results expected are: adaptation and improvement of Energy renovation planning tool and Financial assessment schemes tool, adaptation and/or development of typologies / KPIs databases to regional/local contexts, training materials on the new tools, MoUs in participating territories (including Energy renovation plan for Public-buildings stock, Financial schemes alternatives associated to the plan, and the protocol followed to obtain both of them).During the 1st term of the project an International Webinar was held, participated by all partners, with the objective to present to new territories IMPULSE methodology and the main functionalities of the already available tools. After that, givers and receivers’ partners respectively designed and filled a survey to identify the needs for improvement in the IMPULSE tools, to adapt them to the specific regional/local contexts and to the requirements linked to the new EU programmatic period. The update/improvement of energy renovation and financing planning tools are ongoing and foreseen deadline is the end of December. Receivers’ territories are working to obtain representative databases (typologies/KPIs) of their public building stock, and foreseen deadline is by the end of January. Both activities were initially planned to be finished by the end of November, but there had been some delays mainly related to external services subcontracting procedures. Corrective actions (some tasks are being brought forward AMAP) and new deadlines have been set to make up for lost time and avoid further delays.Regarding communication activities, partners fulfilled the following commitments: project poster produced&showed, IMPULSE PLUS website updated and translated in all national languages (IT and SLO pending to be uploaded), project info available in partners’ corporative websites, 1st newsletter produced (pending the latest additions and launch), project Facebook page updated&promoted, and production of a storytelling video (pending on the national languages subtitles). In addition, all the partners have actively participated in the committee meetings and technical meetings, which are held alternately every 2 months with the aim of continuously monitoring the project activities developed by all partners.
en.expected_outputs: MoU by receivers’ territories on the adoption of the protocol followed for public buildings’ energy renovation planning and financing assessment :4
en.delivered_outputs: Number of available planning tools to monitor and manage energy consumption in public buildings, Number of strategies supporting plans on energy consumption management for public buildings, Number of target groups participating in capacity raising activities on energy efficiency for public buildings, Number of regions and sub-regions engaged (through charters, protocols, MoU) in developing energy efficiency plans/strategies, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 2,

fr.name: Communautés rurales engagées avec l'énergie positive PLUS
fr.description: COMPOSE PLUS capitalisera sur le développement réussi d'un outil de planification du développement durable, l'outil COMPOSE, démontrant sa valeur d'utilisation pratique dans la planification du développement, aidant les décideurs et les planificateurs du développement à mûrir les projets vers la transition énergétique propre, en particulier sur les zones rurales et insulaires de la MED. Il encouragera l'introduction de mesures d'EE et la mise en œuvre des SER comme moyen de fourniture d'énergie ou même de nouveaux modèles commerciaux avec une combinaison d'innovation sociale et technique. COMPOSE PLUS est un consortium de 12 partenaires très motivés de 7 pays MED pour transférer, adopter et intégrer l'outil COMPOSE et augmenter leur capacité de planification et afin la capacité de plusieurs autres parties prenantes. Le transfert sera mis en œuvre par le biais de cours de formation et de renforcement des capacités, co-développés en consortiums, sur la base de l'analyse des besoins des parties prenantes et des nouvelles opportunités de financement du nouveau cadre financier pluriannuel de l'UE. Pour assurer la durabilité, les bénéficiaires signeront un protocole d'accord, pour utiliser davantage l'outil COMPOSE dans leurs opérations, des cours exclusifs de formation des formateurs seront également mis en œuvre non seulement pour assurer la durabilité institutionnelle, mais aussi pour soutenir l'intégration et l'adoption de l'approche COMPOSE dans les pays respectifs.
fr.expected_results: 1 COMPOSE Adoption de l'outil par les organisations respectives : Avec des institutions publiques, particulièrement axées sur les municipalités, ayant la capacité opérationnelle (compréhension et savoir-faire technique / planification) de reconnaître les opportunités de développement local des SER et de l'EE en termes d'économies d'énergie mais aussi d'opportunités socio-économiques, cela conduira par définition à plus / Augmentation de la part des énergies renouvelables provenant de sources locales dans le mix énergétique des zones rurales MED. Jusqu'à 2 événements par type de cours sont prévus dans chacun des 7 pays, permettant une répartition inégale entre eux. Plus de 35 cours seront mis en œuvre, touchant directement plus de 100 décideurs, 200 experts et 100 formateurs potentiels de l'outil COMPOSE.1 COMPOSE Adoption de l'outil par les organisations respectives : Avec des institutions publiques, particulièrement axées sur les municipalités, ayant la capacité opérationnelle (compréhension et savoir-faire technique / planification) de reconnaître les opportunités de développement local des SER et de l'EE en termes d'économies d'énergie mais aussi d'opportunités socio-économiques, cela conduira par définition à plus / Augmentation de la part des énergies renouvelables provenant de sources locales dans le mix énergétique des zones rurales MED. Jusqu'à 2 événements par type de cours sont prévus dans chacun des 7 pays, permettant une répartition inégale entre eux. Plus de 35 cours seront mis en œuvre, touchant directement plus de 100 décideurs, 200 experts et 100 formateurs potentiels de l'outil COMPOSE.
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d'outils de planification visant à développer des plans énergétiques comprenant des sources locales d'énergies renouvelables, Population des îles couverte par des plans, Population des zones rurales couvertes par des plans, Nombre de modèles pour développer des plans d'action comprenant des sources locales d'énergies renouvelables dans l'ensemble du mix énergétique, Nombre de régions et sous-régions engagées (par le biais de chartes, protocoles, MoU) à augmenter la part des sources locales d'énergies renouvelables dans l'ensemble du mix énergétique, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 2,

en.name: Rural Communities engaged with positive energy PLUS
en.description: COMPOSE PLUS project will capitalise on the successful development of a sustainable development planning tool, the COMPOSE tool, demonstrating its practical use value in development planning, supporting policymakers and development planners to mature projects towards the clean energy transition, focusing especially on rural and island areas of the MED. It will promote introduction of EE measures and implementing RES as means of energy supply or even new business models, with a combination of social and technical innovation. COMPOSE PLUS project is a consortium of 12 highly motivated partners from 7 MED countries, to transfer, adopt and mainstream COMPOSE tool and therefore increase their planning capacity and by spill over effect also capacity of several more stakeholders, to be better prepared for absorption of EU and cohesion funds, especially in the energy sector. The transfer will be implemented via training and capacity building courses, co-developed in consortia, upon stakeholder needs analysis and EU new multiannual financial framework financing opportunities. To ensure sustainability, receivers will sign a Memorandum of Understanding, to further use COMPOSE tool in their operations, exclusive train the trainer courses will also be implemented not only to secure institutional sustainability but also to support mainstreaming and further uptake of COMPOSE approach in respective countries.
en.expected_results: 1 COMPOSE Tool uptake by respective organisations : With public institutions, especially focusing on municipalities, having operational capacity (understanding and technical/planning know-how) to recognise local development opportunities of RES and EE in terms of energy savings but also socio-economic opportunities, this will by definition lead to more/increased share of renewable energy from local sources in energy mix of MED rural areas. Up to 2 events per course type are foreseen in each of the 7 countries, allowing an uneven distribution amongst them. Over 35 courses will be implemented, reaching directly over 100 decision makers, 200 experts and 100 potential trainers of the COMPOSE tool.1 COMPOSE Tool uptake by respective organisations : With public institutions, especially focusing on municipalities, having operational capacity (understanding and technical/planning know-how) to recognise local development opportunities of RES and EE in terms of energy savings but also socio-economic opportunities, this will by definition lead to more/increased share of renewable energy from local sources in energy mix of MED rural areas. Up to 2 events per course type are foreseen in each of the 7 countries, allowing an uneven distribution amongst them. Over 35 courses will be implemented, reaching directly over 100 decision makers, 200 experts and 100 potential trainers of the COMPOSE tool.
en.achievements: The COMPOSE Plus is intended to capitalise on the successful sustainable development planning COMPOSE tool, demonstrating its practical use value in development planning, supporting policy makers and development planners to mature projects towards the clean energy transition, focusing especially on rural and island areas of the MED. LP and PPs ensured efficient and effective Management of project activities (A3.1) based on involvement and commitment of 11 partners. LP monitored and guided the work in a transparent way. Online KoM + 3x SCMs were organised. Steering committee (SC) as the main decision-making body of the project was set up and met regularly. Partners were Exchanged information (A3.2) about the project progress regarding to MED Communication Manual, Meeting Minutes, ToDoList, Dropbox. MED web platform was regularly updated via social media, disseminations, presentations, events, articles&press releases and Scientific publication in inter. journal (SPRINGER NATURE). Evaluation of the institutional /administrative needs (A3.3): 224 interested stakeholders have been contacted and responded to the A3.3 survey, declaring in parallel their interest to the toolbox and activities. The analysis of surveys assisted in the evaluation of the institutional& administrative needs and in tailoring the training events and building transferability actions based on stakeholders’ specific needs and potential. The report of key issues and findings from the survey was provided. Updating, upgrading and fine-tuning of the COMPOSE model (A3.4): COMPOSE Toolbox content was updated and renewed. New content making clear links to recent topics, challenges and priorities was developed and uploaded. Technical oriented activities were designed and Toolbox content localisation activities initiated. In Mobilisation of stakeholders in receiving territories (A3.5) 8 individual stakeholder engagement plans were developed to facilitate individual mobilization of important stakeholders needed for implementation of RES and EE measures. They were identified potential stakeholder groups that will help in identification of stakeholders for RES and EE project implementation. In Training and capacity building for stakeholders in the receiving territories (A3.6) useful resources to support tailored training material development were assessed and provided. Tailored capacity building activities in Greece have been identified and 2 experts’ missions have been delivered by partner REAN.
en.delivered_outputs: Number of planning tools to develop energy plans including local RES, Population of islands covered by plans, Population of rural areas covered by plans, Number of models to develop action plans including local RES in energy mix, Number of regions and sub-regions engaged (through charters, protocols, MoU) in increasing share of local RES in energy mix, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 2,

fr.name: Politiques fiscales locales pour l'intégration de l'énergie verte
fr.description: Le projet capitalisera le livrable "Manuel International sur les Modèles de Politiques Fiscalese Écologiques Locales" du projet LOCAL4GREEN. Ce livrable décrit un ensemble de politiques fiscales qui peuvent être mises en œuvre au niveau local pour promouvoir les sources d'énergie renouvelables (SER), en relevant le défi de l'augmentation des SER dans les secteurs non SCEQE grâce à un outil flexible et innovant (fiscalité verte). Par conséquent, le projet LOCAL4GREEN+ se focalise sur l'intégration de ces politiques fiscales locales vertes dans les politiques des nouvelles autorités locales. Le projet mettra en œuvre un processus de formation et adaptera les modèles politiques aux caractéristiques spécifiques des municipalités bénéficiaires en collaboration avec des groupes de travail formés par des décideurs, des responsables politiques et d'autres parties prenantes de ces municipalités. En outre, le projet fournira un soutien technique et mettra en place un processus de liaison stratégique avec les décideurs des municipalités bénéficiaires. Deux anciens partenaires du projet LOCAL4GREEN (FVMP – partenaire principal - et MUSOL) sont impliqués en tant que partenaires donneurs et assureront le transfert des politiques à quatre associations de municipalités impliquées en tant que récepteurs. Les partenaires récepteurs travailleront avec les municipalités partenaires associées qui ont déjà exprimé leur intérêt pour la mise en place d'une fiscalité locale verte.
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d'outils de planification visant à développer des plans énergétiques comprenant des sources locales d'énergies renouvelables, Population des îles couverte par des plans, Population des zones rurales couvertes par des plans, Nombre de modèles pour développer des plans d'action comprenant des sources locales d'énergies renouvelables dans l'ensemble du mix énergétique, Nombre de régions et sous-régions engagées (par le biais de chartes, protocoles, MoU) à augmenter la part des sources locales d'énergies renouvelables dans l'ensemble du mix énergétique, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 2,

en.name: LOCAL Fiscal Policies for GREEN Energy Mainstreaming
en.description: The project will capitalize the deliverable “International Handbook on Green Local Fiscal Policy” of the project LOCAL4GREEN. Such deliverable describes a set of fiscal policies that can be implemented at local level to promote the renewable energy sources (RES), tackling the challenge of increasing RES in the non-ETS sectors through a flexible innovative tool (green taxation). Therefore, the project LOCAL4GREEN+ is a mainstreaming initiative focusing on integrating such green local fiscal policies into the policies of new local authorities. The project will carry out a training process and will adapt the policy models to the specific features of the receivers municipalities in collaboration with working groups formed by decision makers, policy makers and other stakeholders of such municipalities. Furthermore, the project will provide technical support and will deploy a strategic liaising process with the receivers policy makers to ensure the implementation of the green fiscal policies to promote RES. Two former partners of the LOCAL4GREEN project (FVMP – Lead partner - and MUSOL) are involved as givers partners and will ensure the transfer of the policies to four associations of municipalities involved as receivers. The receivers partners will amplify the capitalization process and will work with receivers associated partners municipalities that have already expressed their interest in implementing green local taxation and will concretely implement the capitalized policies.
en.expected_results: 1 Mainstreamed the best practices of the “International Handbook on Green Local Fiscal Policy” into the fiscal policies of new MED municipal : The LOCAL4GREEN selected deliverable offers a wide set of fiscal policies that were successfully tested in several MED municipalities and had a significant impact in terms of promotion of the local renewable energy sources. Such green fiscal policies incentivised taxpayers to modify consume and investment behaviours, opting for renewable energy sources; in other cases, the green fiscal policies enabled the local authorities to increase the tax collection allocating it to renewable energy promotion. Such policies will be adapted and implemented in new municipalities of rural areas and islands of Spain, Italy, Greece, Cyprus and Portugal. Actually, in Spain the FVMP and MUSOL will mainstream the green fiscal policies in new municipalities of the Valencia region and MUSOL also in the Balearic Islands region (new region not covered by the LOCAL4GREEN). The partner AMCAL will mainstream the policies in municipalities of Alentejo Portuguese region, the Italian partner BBI in municipalities of the Friuli and Toscana Italian regions and the partner EFXINI in the Greek regions South Aegean and Epirus (all regions not covered by the LOCAL4GREEN). The UCC will capitalize such policies in new Cyprus rural municipalities, not covered by the LOCAL4GREEN. Even if the project will work both with rural and island municipalities, since only one result indicator can be chosen, it was selected taking into account that most of the local authorities that will mainstream the green fiscal policies are rural.1 Mainstreamed the best practices of the “International Handbook on Green Local Fiscal Policy” into the fiscal policies of new MED municipal : The LOCAL4GREEN selected deliverable offers a wide set of fiscal policies that were successfully tested in several MED municipalities and had a significant impact in terms of promotion of the local renewable energy sources. Such green fiscal policies incentivised taxpayers to modify consume and investment behaviours, opting for renewable energy sources; in other cases, the green fiscal policies enabled the local authorities to increase the tax collection allocating it to renewable energy promotion. Such policies will be adapted and implemented in new municipalities of rural areas and islands of Spain, Italy, Greece, Cyprus and Portugal. Actually, in Spain the FVMP and MUSOL will mainstream the green fiscal policies in new municipalities of the Valencia region and MUSOL also in the Balearic Islands region (new region not covered by the LOCAL4GREEN). The partner AMCAL will mainstream the policies in municipalities of Alentejo Portuguese region, the Italian partner BBI in municipalities of the Friuli and Toscana Italian regions and the partner EFXINI in the Greek regions South Aegean and Epirus (all regions not covered by the LOCAL4GREEN). The UCC will capitalize such policies in new Cyprus rural municipalities, not covered by the LOCAL4GREEN. Even if the project will work both with rural and island municipalities, since only one result indicator can be chosen, it was selected taking into account that most of the local authorities that will mainstream the green fiscal policies are rural.
en.achievements: The Project has carried out the mainstreaming activities as planned in the AF, being some activities and outputs even ahead of schedule. Partners have trained the receiver municipalities which have created the working groups to adapt the green fiscal policies. They are currently working to prepare the viability studies with the concrete and adapted proposals of fiscal policies to promote RES. In some countries the process has been faster and partners have been able to reach substantial progress even drafting the relevant policy acts and supporting the policy makers for their approval. In Spain, 3 municipalities have approved 9 fiscal policies until 31/10/2021 (8 provisionally, 7 of which have been definitively approved in December 2021). Furthermore, according to the advanced situation of the policy act drafts in other receivers municipalities, additional approvals have been produced at the end of 2021, that will duly reported in the final report.Regarding the coordination and management, 2 supporting tools created by LOCAL4GREEN have been updated: the Handbook on Project Management and the Project Monitoring Guide, and also the Project Steering Committee has been created.On 24th March 2021, the Kick-off Meeting was held online due to the pandemic situation and jointly with a training session from the giver partners (FVMP and MUSOL) to receiver partners in order to develop the skills needed to mainstream into new municipalities the green fiscal policies tested by LOCAL4GREEN, included in the “International Handbook on Green Local Fiscal Policy”.In relation to communication, the Project website has been updated with new contents for LOCAL4GREEN PLUS, and the social networks have been nourished with Project activities. A Google drive folder has been created to share with partners the communication pack (logos, poster template, etc) and other issues related to communication, management and mainstreaming. Moreover, partners have collaborated with the RE Community, especially to support the flagship municipalities in Spain and Italy. The adaptation of the green fiscal policies in the RE Community municipalities is still ongoing.The Project has already reached some progress in the outputs 2.1 and 2.3. In total 3 municipalities have approved fiscal policies to promote RES until 31/10/2021 (3 of 12 planned in output 2.1 for June 2022), reaching 54.771 population of rural municipalities (54.771 of 77.265 population planned in output 2.3 for June 2022).
en.expected_outputs: Local and other subnational authorities engaged in implementing local fiscal policies to promote RES :12,Population of islands covered by local fiscal policies that promote RES :21771,Population of rural areas covered by local fiscal policies that promote RES :77265
en.delivered_outputs: Number of planning tools to develop energy plans including local RES, Population of islands covered by plans, Population of rural areas covered by plans, Number of models to develop action plans including local RES in energy mix, Number of regions and sub-regions engaged (through charters, protocols, MoU) in increasing share of local RES in energy mix, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 2,

fr.description: Les décideurs des îles et des zones rurales éprouvent des difficultés à planifier la transition énergétique vers des systèmes énergétiques à faible émissions pour de nombreuses raisons, telles que le manque de connaissances et de données. TRISMI veut relever ce défi en transférant l'outil PRISMI pour augmenter la pénétration des sources d'énergie renouvelables (SER) locales basé sur une analyse multicritères, une approche transnationale, tenant compte des paramètres économiques, techniques et environnementaux, pour identifier des solutions optimales en respectant les spécificités locales. L'approche de transfert reposera sur l'implication des Destinataires permettant une meilleure compréhension de leurs besoins et permettant des ajustements pour une application efficace de l'outil et le développement de 4 Plans Energie Durable et Climat/Agenda de Transition Energétique Propre (pour les îles). Les Donateurs partageront leurs connaissances sur les outils et les Destinataires les informations locales sur le territore et le règlements, en apprenant l’application de l'outil. De plus, le transfert aux Destinataires sera évalué par un test. La collaboration se réalisera par rencontres virtuelles et physiques, et activités communes pour partager expériences/connaissances des participants et une formation individuelle pour les besoins spécifiques. Enfin, les destinataires pourront continuer à diffuser et appliquer l'outil après la durée de vie du projet dans de nouveaux territoires
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d'outils de planification visant à développer des plans énergétiques comprenant des sources locales d'énergies renouvelables, Population des îles couverte par des plans, Population des zones rurales couvertes par des plans, Nombre de modèles pour développer des plans d'action comprenant des sources locales d'énergies renouvelables dans l'ensemble du mix énergétique, Nombre de régions et sous-régions engagées (par le biais de chartes, protocoles, MoU) à augmenter la part des sources locales d'énergies renouvelables dans l'ensemble du mix énergétique, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 2,

en.name: Transferring a toolkit for RES Integration in Smart Mediterranean Islands and rural areas
en.description: Islands and rural areas decision-makers generally find difficulties in planning the transition to low-carbon energy systems for many reasons, such as lack of knowledge and data. TRISMI aims at tackling this challenge by transferring the PRISMI decision-making tool for increasing local Renewable Energy Sources (RES) penetration based on a multicriteria analysis and a transnational approach, considering economic, technical and environmental parameters, to identify optimal solutions respecting local features and social needs. The transferring approach will rely on the involvement of receivers enabling a better understanding of their needs and allowing adjustments for an effective application of the tool and the consequent development of 2 Sustainable Energy and Climate Action Plans (SECAPs) for rural areas and 2 Clean Energy Transition Agendas (CETAs) for islands. Givers will share tool knowledge and receivers will provide information on local contexts, regulations and social needs while learning how to apply the tool. Moreover, the effective transferring to receivers will be assessed by a transferability test. This intertwined collaboration will be realised through virtual and physical meetings, enriched by communal activities that will enable experience sharing and knowledge raising for all participants and one-to-one training to respond to specific needs. Finally, receivers will be able to further disseminate and apply the tool after the project lifetime in new territories.
en.expected_results: 1 Increased capacity for planning of energy transition to sustainable local community using the PRISMI tool : N/A1 Increased capacity for planning of energy transition to sustainable local community using the PRISMI tool : N/A
en.achievements: The PRISMI PLUS project supported islands and rural areas’ decision-makers that generally find difficulties in planning the transition to low-carbon energy systems for many reasons, such as lack of knowledge and data. Indeed, PRISMI PLUS worked towards tackling this challenge by transferring the PRISMI decision-making tool for increasing local Renewable Energy Sources (RES) penetration based on a multicriteria analysis and a transnational approach, considering economic, technical and environmental parameters, to identify optimal solutions respecting local features and social needs. The transferring approach relied on the involvement of receivers enabling a better understanding of their needs and the consequent development of 2 Sustainable Energy and Climate Action Plans (SECAPs) for rural areas and 2 Clean Energy Transition Agendas (CETAs) for islands. This was possible thanks to the intertwined collaboration between technical and institutional partners realised through virtual and physical meetings, enriched by communal activities that enabled experience sharing and knowledge raising for all participants. In the end, receivers were put in the position of being able to further disseminate and apply the tool after the project lifetime in new territories. In order to also enlarge the audience for a further use of the toolkit after the project end, the guidelines and documentation have been translated in different languages and also video tutorial have been developed in different languages. Furthermore, the toolkit was applied also on 6 additional Flagship Cases selected in collaboration with the Thematic Community that offered the opportunity to test the toolkit on more and different case studies and contexts proving once more the toolkit flexibility.
en.expected_outputs: PRISMI Tool application for RES integration :6,Developed SECAP (for rural areas) and CETA Clean Energy Transition Agendas (for islands) :4,Number of territories that received training :10,Population in rural areas covered by the trained organisations :110000,Population in islands covered by the trained organisations :100000
en.delivered_outputs: Number of planning tools to develop energy plans including local RES, Population of islands covered by plans, Population of rural areas covered by plans, Number of models to develop action plans including local RES in energy mix, Number of regions and sub-regions engaged (through charters, protocols, MoU) in increasing share of local RES in energy mix, Expenditures declared to the EC for Axis 2,

fr.name: Transfert de l'outil d'aide à la décision PrioritEE aux autorités publiques de l'espace MED
fr.description: Les bâtiments publics (BP) représentent 10% du parc immobilier total et consomment de grandes quantités d'énergie avec les émissions de GES associées. Bien que la rénovation des BP soit stratégique pour parvenir à une économie sans carbone comme le prévoit le Green Deal de l’UE, elle est entravée par des compétences insuffisantes des autorités publiques, une connaissance des mesures et un manque de ressources financières.PrioritEE PLUS vise à capitaliser sur l'expérience de PrioritEE et à améliorer, grâce à la coopération transnationale, les capacités des pouvoirs publics dans la gestion énergétique des BP et dans la planification locale des énergies durables. Il favorisera l'utilisation d'outils analytiques pour des décisions éclairées et la promotion de solutions techniques réplicables dans différents territoires MED.Les fonctionnalités du PrioritEE DST seront améliorées pour faciliter son application entre les différents niveaux des agences et pour soutenir les plans d'action locaux. Un processus structuré de formation et de transfert du DST permettra d'améliorer les compétences du personnel municipaux pour réduire la consommation d'énergie, améliorer le confort thermique du parc BP et sélectionner les bâtiments sur lesquels il est économiquement viable et écologiquement rationnel d'intervenir.Les principaux acteurs locaux soutiendront le processus de transfert et contribueront à la définition de recommandations pour les stratégies et plans locaux.
fr.expected_results: 1 Protocole d'accord sur l'adoption de DSTool : NA1 Protocole d'accord sur l'adoption de DSTool : NA
fr.expected_outputs: Protocole d'accord sur l'adoption de DST :3
fr.delivered_outputs: Nombre d'outils de planification disponibles pour le suivi et la gestion de la consommation énergétique dans les bâtiments publics, Nombre de stratégies en faveur des plans de gestion de la consommation énergétique dans les bâtiments publics, Nombre de groupes cibles participant aux activités de sensibilisation sur l'efficacité énergétique pour les bâtiments publics, Nombre de régions et sous-régions engagées (par le biais de chartes, protocoles, MoU) à développer des plans/stratégies d'efficacité énergétique, Dépenses déclarées à la CE pour Axe 2,

en.name: Transferring the PrioritEE Decision Support Tool to public authorities in the MED area
en.description: Public Buildings (PBs) represent 10% of the overall building stock, due to their specificities they consume large amounts of energy with associated GHGs emissions. Although PBs’ renovation is strategic to achieve a carbon free economy as foreseen by the EU Green Deal, this is hampered by insufficient competences of public authorities, knowledge on measures and lack of financial resources. PrioritEE PLUS aims to capitalize on the experience of the PrioritEE project and improve, through transnational cooperation, the capacities of public authorities in the energy management of PBs and in local sustainable energy planning. It will favor the use of analytical tools for informed decisions and the promotion of replicable technical solutions in different MED territories.The features of the PrioritEE Decision Support Tool (DST) will be further improved to facilitate its application among different governmental levels (e.g. municipalities, energy agencies, public institutions) and support local action plans (e.g. SECAP). A structured training and transferring process based on the DST will improve the skills of municipal staff and technicians to reduce energy consumption, improve thermal comfort in the PB stock and select the buildings on which it is economically viable and environmentally sound to intervene. Key local actors will support the transferring process through a multi-level communication and contribute to the definition of recommendations for local strategies and plans.
en.expected_results: 1 MoUs on DST uptake : The main result of the project will be an effective transfer of the improved PrioritEE DST (here named PrioritEE4MED DST) and its concrete uptake by the competent local governments, through the development of the ad-hoc Memorandum of Understanding (MoUs). This will be the successful conclusion of the planned articulated transfer process, which will be based on on-line and in-person capacity building activities focused on the DST in a modular structure, with self-supporting chapters in online learning to take into account any social restrictions imposed by the COVID-19 pandemic.The resulting MoUs, at least three (3), developed with the 3 main “receiver” partners will testify the contribution of the PrioritEE4MED project to the programme result indicator “Share of sub-regional and local energy efficiency plans including adapted measures for public building stock”.1 MoUs on DST uptake : The main result of the project will be an effective transfer of the improved PrioritEE DST (here named PrioritEE4MED DST) and its concrete uptake by the competent local governments, through the development of the ad-hoc Memorandum of Understanding (MoUs). This will be the successful conclusion of the planned articulated transfer process, which will be based on on-line and in-person capacity building activities focused on the DST in a modular structure, with self-supporting chapters in online learning to take into account any social restrictions imposed by the COVID-19 pandemic.The resulting MoUs, at least three (3), developed with the 3 main “receiver” partners will testify the contribution of the PrioritEE4MED project to the programme result indicator “Share of sub-regional and local energy efficiency plans including adapted measures for public building stock”.
